steps.title: Set up equations from the geometry
steps.content:
For a right rectangular prism with a square base, let $s$ = side of the square base and $h$ = height.

The volume is the base area times height: $$s^2 h = 539$$
Each of the four lateral faces is a rectangle with dimensions $s \times h$, so each face has area: $$s h = 77$$

steps.title: Divide the equations to find s
steps.content:
Divide the volume equation by the lateral face equation: $$\frac{s^2 h}{s h} = \frac{539}{77}$$
The variables $s$ and $h$ cancel on the left, leaving: $$s = \frac{539}{77} = 7$$

steps.title: Substitute to find the height
steps.content:
Now substitute $s = 7$ into the lateral face equation $s h = 77$: $$7h = 77$$
Divide both sides by 7: $$h = 11$$
That is the correct answer.
